sections.title: Etiquette
sections.summary: Politeness, punctuality, and respect for age or status matter in many interactions.
tips: Use both hands when giving or receiving important items.

sections.title: Honorifics
sections.summary: Korean language uses honorific levels that reflect social distance, age, and context.
tips: Use polite speech until invited otherwise.
Job titles can be used instead of first names.

sections.title: Food Culture
sections.summary: Meals are often shared, with side dishes, soups, rice, grilled meats, stews, and cafe culture.
tips: Wait briefly for seniors to start in formal meals.

sections.title: Festivals
sections.summary: Important holidays include Seollal, Chuseok, Buddha's Birthday, and many local cultural festivals.
tips: Travel can be crowded during Chuseok and Seollal.
University festivals are major student events.

sections.title: School Culture
sections.summary: Education is structured and competitive, with strong emphasis on attendance, exams, and group projects.

sections.title: Workplace Culture
sections.summary: Workplaces vary by company, but hierarchy, communication style, and team fit remain important.
tips: Korean ability improves trust and workplace range.
